data.name: Text_NoMonitorsDiscovered
data.comment: Text-mode output when list/get finds no monitors.

data.name: Text_NotSupported
data.comment: Text-mode marker for an unsupported setting.

data.name: Text_Unknown
data.comment: Text-mode marker for a supported setting whose value was not read.

data.name: Text_NoProfilesSaved
data.comment: profiles command: no saved profiles exist.

data.name: Text_NoValuesReported
data.comment: capabilities: a discrete VCP code advertised no enumerated values.

data.name: Text_ProfileProcessedBestEffort
data.comment: {0} = profile name. apply-profile is best-effort and must not claim hardware success; per-setting outcomes are not reported.

data.name: Warn_MonitorNumberIgnored
data.value: warning: --monitor-number {0} ignored because --monitor-id was also provided
data.comment: {0} = the complete comma-separated list of ignored monitor numbers. Flag names are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_NoSettingSpecified
data.value: no setting specified; pass one of --brightness/--contrast/--volume/--color-temperature/--input-source/--power-state/--orientation
data.comment: The flag names are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_OnlyOneSetting
data.value: only one setting may be applied per 'set' call
data.comment: 'set' is a literal command name and must not be translated.

data.name: Hint_OnlyOneSetting
data.value: split into multiple invocations: one --<setting> per call

data.name: Error_UnknownSetting
data.comment: {0} = the setting name the user passed to --setting.

data.name: Hint_ValidSettings
data.comment: {0} = comma-separated canonical setting names (CLI syntax, not translated).

data.name: Error_TimedOut
data.comment: {0} = number of seconds.

data.name: Error_UnexpectedError
data.comment: {0} = exception message.

data.name: Error_ProviderUnavailable
data.value: PowerDisplay is not running. Enable it in PowerToys settings.
data.comment: Shown when the CLI cannot reach the PowerDisplay app over the IPC pipe.

data.name: Error_DeserializeMismatch
data.value: Response could not be deserialized as expected type.
data.comment: Shown when the app's IPC response does not match the CLI's expected schema (version skew).

data.name: Error_NegativeStep
data.comment: --step is CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_NoAdjustSettingSpecified
data.value: no setting specified; pass one of --brightness/--contrast/--volume
data.comment: The flag names are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_InvalidMonitorNumber
data.value: invalid monitor number(s) '{0}'; use comma-separated 1-based indices, e.g. 1,2,3
data.comment: {0} = the raw value the user passed to -n/--monitor-number. -n/--monitor-number and the example indices are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Error_SingleMonitorOnly
data.value: this command accepts a single monitor; comma-separated monitor numbers are only supported by set/up/down
data.comment: set/up/down are CLI command names and must not be translated.

data.name: Label_Error
data.comment: Prefix label for an error line, e.g. "Error: unknown setting foo".

data.name: Label_Monitor
data.comment: Label for the monitor line under an error, e.g. "monitor: 1 (Dell U2720Q)".

data.name: Label_Expected
data.comment: Label for the expected-value line under an error.

data.name: Label_Supported
data.comment: Label for the supported-values line under an error.

data.name: Label_Diagnostic
data.comment: Label for a low-level technical diagnostic line under an error (e.g. a VCP capability reason or a driver error string, shown verbatim in English).

data.name: Label_Hint
data.comment: Label for the hint line under an error.

data.name: Text_ExpectedInteger
data.comment: {0} = an inclusive numeric range like "[0, 100]" (not translated). Shown on the "expected" line for a numeric out-of-range error.

data.name: ErrMsg_OutOfRange
data.comment: {0} = the value the user passed; {1} = the setting name (e.g. brightness). Neither is translated.

data.name: ErrMsg_InvalidInteger
data.value: {0} is not a valid integer for {1}
data.comment: {0} = the value the user passed; {1} = the setting name. Neither is translated.

data.name: ErrMsg_InvalidDiscrete
data.value: {0} is not a valid value for {1}
data.comment: {0} = the value the user passed; {1} = the setting name. Neither is translated.

data.name: ErrMsg_DiscreteNotInSet
data.value: {0} is not in the supported set for {1}
data.comment: {0} = the value the user passed; {1} = the setting name. The supported values are listed on a separate line.

data.name: ErrMsg_InvalidOrientation
data.comment: {0} = the value the user passed (not translated).

data.name: ErrMsg_Unsupported
data.comment: {0} = the setting name (e.g. volume), not translated.

data.name: ErrMsg_PowerBlankingConfirm
data.value: this power state blanks the display

data.name: ErrMsg_UnknownSetting
data.comment: {0} = the setting name the user passed (not translated).

data.name: ErrMsg_NotDiscreteSetting
data.comment: {0} = the setting name the user passed to --setting (not translated).

data.name: ErrMsg_MonitorNotFoundNumber
data.comment: {0} = the 1-based monitor number the user passed (not translated).

data.name: ErrMsg_MonitorNotFoundId
data.comment: {0} = the monitor id the user passed (not translated).

data.name: ErrMsg_NotAdjustable
data.comment: {0} = the setting name (not translated). Shown for up/down on a non-continuous setting.

data.name: ErrMsg_AdjustValueUnknown
data.value: the current {0} value could not be read
data.comment: {0} = the setting name (not translated). Shown when up/down cannot read the starting value.

data.name: ErrMsg_ProfileNotFound
data.comment: {0} = the profile id

data.name: ErrMsg_UnknownCommand
data.comment: {0} = the command name (not translated).

data.name: Hint_ValidDiscreteSettings
data.comment: {0} = comma-separated discrete setting names (CLI syntax, not translated).

data.name: Hint_AdjustSettings
data.comment: {0} = comma-separated continuous setting names (CLI syntax, not translated).

data.name: Hint_UseSetForAbsolute
data.value: use 'PowerToys.PowerDisplay.Cli.exe set' to assign an absolute value
data.comment: 'PowerToys.PowerDisplay.Cli.exe set' is a literal command and must not be translated.

data.name: Hint_UseHexVcp
data.value: use a hex VCP value (0x??); run 'PowerToys.PowerDisplay.Cli.exe capabilities' to list supported values
data.comment: The command and 0x?? are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Hint_RunList
data.value: run 'PowerToys.PowerDisplay.Cli.exe list' to see available monitors
data.comment: 'PowerToys.PowerDisplay.Cli.exe list' is a literal command and must not be translated.

data.name: Hint_SelectorMissing
data.value: specify --monitor-number/-n or --monitor-id/-i; run 'PowerToys.PowerDisplay.Cli.exe list' to see available monitors
data.comment: The option and command names are CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Hint_Orientation
data.comment: The degree values must not be translated.

data.name: Hint_ConfirmPowerOff
data.value: use --confirm-power-off to allow power states that blank the display
data.comment: --confirm-power-off is CLI syntax and must not be translated.

data.name: Hint_RunProfiles
data.value: run 'PowerToys.PowerDisplay.Cli.exe profiles' to see available profiles
data.comment: 'PowerToys.PowerDisplay.Cli.exe profiles' is a literal command and must not be translated.
